volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Trojan Women (Section 1.176 - Section 315)

sections.label: Section 1.176
sections.translation: With trembling step , alas ! I leave this tent of Agamemnon to learn of you , my royal mistress , whether the Argives have resolved to take my wretched life , or

sections.label: Section 1.180
sections.translation: whether the sailors at the prow are making ready to ply their oars .

sections.label: Section 1.182
sections.translation: My child , your wakeful heart !

sections.label: Section 1.183
sections.translation: I have come , stricken with terror . Has a herald from the Danaids already arrived ?

sections.label: Section 1.185
sections.translation: To whom am I , poor captive , given as a slave ?

sections.label: Section 1.186
sections.translation: You are not far from being allotted now .

sections.label: Section 1.187
sections.translation: Alas ! What man of Argos or Phthia will bear me in sorrow far from Troy , to his home , or to some island fastness ?

sections.label: Section 1.190
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! Whose slave shall I become in my old age ? in what land ? a poor old drone , the wretched copy of a corpse , alas ! set to keep the gate

sections.label: Section 1.195
sections.translation: or tend their children , I who once held royal rank in Troy .

sections.label: Section 2.197
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! What piteous dirge will you devise to mourn the outrage done you ? No more through Ida ’ s looms

sections.label: Section 2.200
sections.translation: shall I ply the shuttle to and fro . I look my last on my children ’ s bodies , my last ; I shall endure surpassing misery , it may be as the unwilling bride of some Hellene ( perish the night and fortune that brings me to this !);

sections.label: Section 2.205
sections.translation: it may be as a wretched slave from Peirene ’ s sacred fount I shall draw their store of water . Oh ! may it be ours to come to Theseus ’ famous realm , a land of joy .

sections.label: Section 2.210
sections.original: μὴ γὰρ δὴ δίναν γʼ Εὐρώτα ,
sections.translation: Never , never let me see Eurotas ’ swirling tide , hateful home of Helen , there to meet and be the slave of Menelaus , whose hand laid Troy waste !

sections.label: Section 2.214
sections.translation: That holy land by Peneus fed ,

sections.label: Section 2.215
sections.translation: nestling in all its beauty at Olympus ’ foot , is said , so have I heard , to be a very granary of wealth and teeming fruitfulness ; next to the sacred soil of Theseus , I could wish to reach that land .

sections.label: Section 2.220
sections.translation: They tell me too Hephaestus ’ home , beneath the shadow of Aetna , fronting Phoenicia , the mother of Sicilian hills , is famous for the crowns it gives to valor . Or may I find a home on that shore which lies very near

sections.label: Section 2.225
sections.translation: Ionia ’ s sea , a land watered by Crathis , lovely stream , that dyes the hair an auburn tint , feeding with its holy waves and making glad the home of heroes .

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Δαναῶν ὅδʼ ἀπὸ στρατιᾶς
sections.translation: But see ! a herald from the army of Danaids , with a store of fresh proclamations , comes hastening here . What is his errand ? What does he say ? For we are indeed slaves now to Dorian lords .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: Ἑκάβη , πυκνὰς γὰρ οἶσθά μʼ ἐς Τροίαν ὁδοὺς
sections.translation: Hecuba , you know me from my many journeys to and fro as herald between the Achaean army and Troy ; I was no stranger to you , lady , even before : I , Talthybius , now sent with a fresh message .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.translation: Ah , kind friends , it has come ! what I so long have dreaded .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: ἤδη κεκλήρωσθʼ , εἰ τόδʼ ἦν ὑμῖν φόβος .
sections.translation: The lot has decided your fates already , if that was what you feared .

sections.label: Section 241
sections.translation: Ah me ! What city did you say , Thessalian , Phthian , or Cadmean ?

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: κατʼ ἄνδρʼ ἑκάστη κοὐχ ὁμοῦ λελόγχατε .
sections.translation: Each warrior took his prize in turn ; you were not all at once assigned .

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: τίνʼ ἄρα τίς ἔλαχε ; τίνα πότμος εὐτυχὴς
sections.translation: To whom has the lot assigned us severally ? Which of us Trojan women

sections.label: Section 245
sections.translation: does a happy fortune await ?

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: οἶδʼ · ἀλλʼ ἕκαστα πυνθάνου , μὴ πάνθʼ ὁμοῦ .
sections.translation: I know , but ask your questions separately , not all at once .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.translation: Then tell me , whose prize is my daughter , hapless Cassandra ?

sections.label: Section 249
sections.translation: King Agamemnon has chosen her out for himself .

sections.label: Section 250
sections.translation: To be the slave-girl of his Spartan wife ? Ah me !

sections.label: Section 252
sections.translation: No , to share with him his stealthy love .

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: ἦ τὰν τοῦ Φοίβου παρθένον , ᾇ γέρας ὁ
sections.translation: What ! Phoebus ’ virgin-priestess , to whom the god with golden locks granted the gift of maidenhood ?

sections.label: Section 255
sections.translation: The dart of love has pierced his heart , love for the frenzied maid .

sections.label: Section 256
sections.translation: Daughter , cast from you the sacred keys , and from your body tear the holy wreaths that drape you in their folds .

sections.label: Section 259
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ μέγʼ αὐτῇ βασιλικῶν λέκτρων τυχεῖν ;
sections.translation: Why ! is it not an honor that she should win our monarch ’ s love ?

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: τί δʼ ὃ νεοχμὸν ἀπʼ ἐμέθεν ἐλάβετε τέκος , ποῦ μοι ;
sections.translation: What have you done to her whom recently you took from me , my child ?

sections.label: Section 262
sections.translation: Do you mean Polyxena , or whom do you inquire about ?

sections.label: Section 263
sections.translation: Yes , that one ; to whom has the lot assigned her ?

sections.label: Section 264
sections.translation: To minister at Achilles ’ tomb has been appointed her .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.translation: Woe is me ! I the mother of a dead man ’ s slave ! What custom , what ordinance is this among Hellenes , friend ?

sections.label: Section 268
sections.translation: Count your daughter happy ; it is well with her .

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: τί τόδʼ ἔλακες ; ἆρά μοι ἀέλιον λεύσσει ;
sections.translation: What wild words are these ? Please tell me , is she still alive ?

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ἔχει πότμος νιν , ὥστʼ ἀπηλλάχθαι πόνων .
sections.translation: Her fate is one that sets her free from trouble .

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: τί δʼ ἁ τοῦ χαλκεομήστορος Ἕκτορος δάμαρ ,
sections.translation: And what of the wife of Hector skilled in arms , sad Andromache ? declare her fate .

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: καὶ τήνδʼ Ἀχιλλέως ἔλαβε παῖς ἐξαίρετον .
sections.translation: She too was a chosen prize ; Achilles ’ son took her .

sections.label: Section 274
sections.translation: As for me

sections.label: Section 275
sections.translation: whose hair is white with age , who need to hold a staff to be to me a third foot , whose servant am I to be ?

sections.label: Section 277
sections.original: Ἰθάκης Ὀδυσσεὺς ἔλαχʼ ἄναξ δούλην σʼ ἔχειν .
sections.translation: Odysseus , king of Ithaca , has taken you to be his slave .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! Now smite the close-shorn head !

sections.label: Section 280
sections.translation: tear your cheeks with your nails ! Ah me ! I have fallen as a slave to a treacherous foe I hate , a monster of lawlessness ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ὃς πάντα τἀκεῖθεν ἐνθάδ ε στρέφει , τὰ δʼ
sections.translation: one that by his double tongue has turned against us all that once was friendly in his camp , changing this for that and that for this again . Oh weep for me , you Trojan women ! Lost and ill-fated !

sections.label: Section 290
sections.translation: Ah woe ! a victim to a most unhappy lot !

sections.label: Section 292
sections.original: τὸ μὲν σὸν οἶσθα , πότνια , τὰς δʼ ἐμὰς τύχας
sections.translation: Your fate , royal mistress , now you know ; but for me , what Helene or Achaean is master of my destiny ?

sections.label: Section 294
sections.translation: Go , servants , and bring Cassandra forth to me here

sections.label: Section 295
sections.translation: at once , that I may place her in our captain ’ s hands , and then conduct to the rest of the chiefs the captives each has had assigned . Ha ! what is the blaze of torches there within ? What are they doing ? Are they firing the chambers ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.translation: because they must leave this land and be carried away to Argos ? Are they setting themselves aflame in their longing for death ? Truly the free bear their troubles in cases like this with a stiff neck . Open up ! lest their deed , which suits them well

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: ἐχθρὸν δʼ Ἀχαιοῖς εἰς ἔμʼ αἰτίαν βάλῃ .
sections.translation: but finds small favor with the Achaeans , bring blame on me .

sections.label: Section 306
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , οὐ πιμπρᾶσιν , ἀλλὰ παῖς ἐμὴ
sections.translation: It is not that they are setting anything ablaze , but my child Cassandra , frenzied maid , comes rushing wildly here .

sections.label: Section 308
sections.translation: Bring the light , uplift and show its flame ! I am doing the god ’ s service , see ! see ! making his shrine to glow with tapers bright .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.translation: O Hymen , lord of marriage ! blessed is the bridegroom ; blessed am I also , soon to wed a princely lord in Argos . Hail Hymen , lord of marriage !

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ἐπεὶ σύ , μᾶτερ , ἐπὶ δάκρυσι καὶ
sections.translation: Since you , my mother , are busied with tears and lamentations in your mourning for my father ’ s death and for our country dear , I at my own nuptials
